reviews.review_msg: Bob is a funny guy, most people compare him to Eeyore based on his demeanour. I took this class in the summer so it was a bit odd, but overall he is a really nice prof and tries to make his lectures interesting. if you can take a class with him i highly recommend it hes very knowledgeable and has been around a while, he knows what hes talking about

Professor Muir was very kind and easy to reach out to. He responds to emails quickly and gives helpful feedback, if you ask for it. Read the rubric carefully for each assignment, pay attention to his slides/videos (majority of exam material is from there, not the textbook). It's definitely a GPA booster class, I enjoyed it and recommend it!

Bob Muir is an excellent professor! I have taken numerous ARCH courses taught by him from the 100 level to the 400 level. He is one of the best professors in the SFU Archaeology department, and his lectures are really interesting to sit through. 100% recommended!

TA's mark the assignments very harshly. They will deduct significant marks if you have improper referencing. This prof on the other hand, his always grumpy for some reason and deliberately wants to catch his students cheating. He would generate AI made papers for himself and would then cross check your assignment to see if they match.

I have taken many classes with him over my degree.
His lectures are where I have learned practical information that is applicable to a career, compared to some courses that seem like u are being set up to stay in academia.
Assignments are fairly graded and helped me learn the course content. Exams always reflect what we learned in class.

Bob is a great lecturer. Many struggle to see the charm and passion through the monotone voice but he genuinely cares about everyone doing well in his class. He does often look down when lecturing and does warn his students about this which I think is perfectly fine as he is more focused on conveying the material in a clear and understandable way.

Bob can be VERY hard to pay attention to because he's very monotone and doesn't look up for questions but don't be afraid to just speak out. I have always found him very lenient and helpful. He can actually be a lot of fun when he is comfortable so don't be turned off from his seemingly grouchy mood.

Lectures are very low-energy, if you raise your hand with a question he'll never see it, you have to verbally interrupt because he doesn't look up when he talks. It's not very engaging. If you make the effort to get to know him I think he is better, but that seems to be hard to do. He is definitely very knowledgeable about PNW archaeology.
Course assignments and readings are very practical and relevant. Challenging assignments.

took arch101 intersession & found it pretty easy, asynch w weekly discussions worth 35% & 3 fairly short assignments worth a total of 65%. bob is easy to reach out to, replies to emails quickly, & provides good feedback. weekly films were assigned which I enjoyed bc they were really interesting! def recommend this class for b-soc, good gpa booster
